title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition – As of March 31, 2026
content:
The credit union's loan portfolio remains actively managed and appropriately reserved, though overall loan balances declined -7.9% from the previous period.

Total loans of $20,708 thousand remain concentrated in used vehicle and new vehicle segments, which continue to represent the primary sources of both earnings and credit risk. Delinquent loans fell 17.4%, representing a decrease from the previous quarter.

Portfolio Composition:
The credit union's loan portfolio continues to be primarily concentrated in consumer lending categories. As of March 31, 2026, new vehicle totaled $5,939 thousand (28.7%) and used vehicle totaled $6,988 thousand (33.7%), together representing approximately 62.4% of the total portfolio.

Portfolio Composition:
The remaining segments include all other unsecured at $5,215 thousand (25.2%). Overall loan balances declined -7.9% from the previous period, reflecting continued balance-sheet contraction driven by reductions in consumer auto lending categories.

Delinquency & Credit Quality:Credit performance remains manageable despite modest delinquency levels within certain segments. Total delinquent loans fell 17.4%, representing a decrease of 27.0% from the previous quarter. The largest delinquency exposure remains in used vehicle ($6,988) and new vehicle ($5,939), followed by all other unsecured ($5,215).

Delinquency & Credit Quality:
The concentration of delinquencies within consumer auto segments remains consistent with the overall portfolio composition. Management continues to monitor delinquency migration trends and collection performance to limit future credit deterioration.
